title: Post-mortem: checkout 500s on Valentine's Day
body: On Feb 14 the checkout service saw a 12-minute spike to 8% error rate. Root cause: a feature flag for a promotional banner was enabled without the backing config, causing a nil dereference in the cart view for ~3% of requests. Fix: config deployed + flag toggle added to the pre-deploy checklist. Lesson: feature flags need a 'config prerequisite' field.

title: Post-mortem: inventory query latency
body: Inventory p99 latency went from 120ms to 900ms for about an hour on Jan 29. Root cause: a batch reconciliation job was accidentally scheduled for 10am instead of 2am, competing with live traffic for DB IOPS. Fix: job rescheduled, alerting added for unexpected batch job activity during business hours.

title: Correlating incidents with deploys
body:
First question for any incident: did something deploy recently?

Rule of thumb: if an alert fires within 20 minutes of a deploy to the same service, the deploy is the prime suspect until proven otherwise. Check the changelog, check whether the error signature matches the changed code paths.

Exception: dependency alerts. If service A is alerting but service B just deployed and A depends on B, check B first.
